title: Histology CORE Lab Standard Operating Procedures
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"afo3q","text":"This standard operating procedure (SOP) outlines the standardized start-of-day practices for the UVA Histology Research CORE Laboratory. It is designed to ensure consistency, reliability, and safety in the preparation, processing, and staining of biological tissue samples. This SOP is intended to promote high-quality results and minimize variability between employees.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"5cflh","text":"- Flotation bath","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"mqdw0","text":"- Automated H\u0026E stainer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9t9wp","text":"- Paraffin embedding station","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ei3ai","text":"- Tissue processors (three units)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aoeua","text":"- -80°C freezer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ryk3","text":"- Slide heating and drying oven (oven)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"831nx","text":"- Paraffin dispenser","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Fill all flotation baths intended for use that day with DI water.
Remove the covers from the automated H&E stainer and check all reagent levels. Refill any reagents that are underfilled to the appropriate volume.
Check the oven for slides awaiting H&E staining. Load any prepared slides into the automated H&E stainer.
Check the status of all three of the tissue processors. Note any alerts or issues and report them to the Lab Manager if needed.
Turn on the paraffin embedding station to allow it to reach working temperature for tissue embedding.
Check the paraffin wax and reagent levels of the tissue processors, paraffin embedding station, and paraffin dispenser. Refill as needed.
Verify that the temperature of the -80°C freezer is within the acceptable range of -68°C to -80°C. Report any deviations immediately to the Lab Manager.
creator: Taylor Curro
